twitter.post:
Here is the latest in finance news.
• Fed Chair Warsh vows to keep fighting for 2% inflation
• Trump drops the proposed fee on cargo ships in the Strait
• New files link Commerce Secretary Lutnick to Epstein HEADLINNE.com #Finance #Economy

Your tech update for today
• Apple's new AI Siri is now open for public beta testing
• The UK is considering a midnight social media curfew for teens

linkedin.title: Most news feeds are broken because they treat everyone the same.
linkedin.body:
When you open a standard news app, you are usually looking at the same top stories as millions of other people. It assumes that because something is popular, it must be relevant to you. But your interests are specific. Your time is valuable. And you shouldn't have to sift through dozens of headlines just to find the one story that actually matters to your life or your work.

True personalization isn't about creating an echo chamber. It is about removing the noise. It is about using technology to understand what you care about and filtering out the rest. When news is tailored to the individual, you spend less time scrolling and more time actually learning.

We built Headlinne because we believe news should work for the reader, not the other way around. It is a simple shift, but it changes how you consume information entirely.
linkedin.cta: If you are tired of the clutter, come see a better way to stay informed at HEADLINNE.com.

instagram.title: Top 5 Finance Stories Today

slides.headline: Fed Chief Warsh on Inflation
slides.explanation: Kevin Warsh appeared before the House Financial Services committee today. He promised to stay focused on bringing inflation back down to the 2% target.

slides.headline: Trump Drops Hormuz Cargo Fee
slides.explanation: President Trump backed away from his plan to charge a 20% fee on ships using the Strait of Hormuz. The US continues its efforts to block Iranian control of the waterway.

slides.headline: New Report on Lutnick and Epstein
slides.explanation: A British whistleblower found links between US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick and Jeffrey Epstein. These details were uncovered within public documents.

slides.headline: Warsh Promises Policy Shift
slides.explanation: Kevin Warsh vowed to change how the Federal Reserve handles the economy. He wants to fix the inflation issues that have impacted Americans for the last five years.

slides.headline: Buffett Halts Charity Donations
slides.explanation: Warren Buffett has stopped donating to the Bill Gates charity. The decision follows recent congressional hearings involving Gates and his past connections to Jeffrey Epstein.

instagram.caption: Here are the top five finance stories you need to know today. From Federal Reserve updates to major shifts in corporate donations, we have the latest. Stay informed with Headlinne. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.

instagram.title: Top 5 Things In Tech Today

slides.headline: Try Apple's New AI Siri Today
slides.explanation: You can now test out the new AI-powered Siri features on your iPhone. Apple just released the iOS 27 public beta, so you don't need to be a developer to get an early look.

slides.headline: UK Teens May Face Social Media Curfews
slides.explanation: New proposals suggest a midnight curfew for older teens on social media apps. Critics argue the plan is limited since teens can choose to opt out of the restrictions.

slides.headline: OpenAI Might Be Building a Smart Speaker
slides.explanation: Reports suggest OpenAI is working on a voice-controlled device for chatting with ChatGPT. It likely won't have a screen but will use cameras and sensors to understand its surroundings.

slides.headline: New $2B AI Drug Startup in the Works
slides.explanation: Lightspeed is in discussions to lead the funding round, sources say.

slides.headline: OpenAI's Device Could Be a Moving Speaker
slides.explanation: The rumored OpenAI hardware might include mechanical parts that move on their own. The goal is to make the device feel more like a physical companion rather than just a speaker.

instagram.caption: The tech world is moving fast today, from new Apple updates to potential hardware shifts at OpenAI. Here is your quick breakdown of the biggest headlines you need to know. Swipe through to catch up on the latest developments. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.
